title: Senior Site Reliability Engineer
score_reasoning: Your Python expertise, containerization, and infrastructure experience (Docker, AWS, Terraform) provide a solid foundation for this SRE role, and the globally remote setup with travel aligns with your preferences. However, this is fundamentally a different specialization than your stated backend engineering focus: the role is operations/infrastructure-focused rather than microservices architecture and application development. Your CV shows backend application engineering with some infrastructure tooling, not deep SRE/platform operations experience. While the Python proficiency and Linux familiarity are transferable strengths, the role's emphasis on bare-metal networking, kernel work, OpenStack, and Kubernetes (which you lack) represents a meaningful shift away from your core distributed systems architect path. This reads more as a 'sideways' move than a growth trajectory toward senior backend leadership in high-scale microservices.

red_flags: No Kubernetes experience (critical for modern SRE roles)
Limited deep operations/DevOps background in CV
Role focuses on infrastructure ops rather than backend application architecture
Not aligned with stated career goal of deepening backend distributed systems expertise
